title: dryopteris raddeana
intro: Dryopteris raddeana, also known as Radde's wood fern or Chinese wood fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to China and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In herbal preparations, Dryopteris raddeana is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being combined with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine formulas to enhance its medicinal properties.
